summary: Android App Tabbing Persistence

description.text:
### Tested versions

Godot Android 4.4 and 4.5.0.dev

### System information

Android 10 Smartphone
Updated: Huawei P30 Lite

### Issue description

Godot on Android lacks persistence between app tabbing. If the app is switched, like visiting file manager, browser, etc, then switching back to Godot, the editor reloads with the splash screen, then reloads the scene from where the user left off. This is quirky, because sometimes if a user goes away for even a few seconds, the editor reloads from splash, but sometimes the user can be on another app for quite some time, and the editor window is still there without the splash and last scene reloading everytime when the user switches back

### Steps to reproduce

Launch a scene.
Tab away to another app for a few seconds.
Tab back to the editor.
Try these steps for a few seconds, for around 10 seconds, and around 30 seconds or more.
Repeat a few times after editing your test scene.

summary: Error in .gd file with appearance of GDScript_evgvs

description.text:
### Tested versions

v4.5.dev4.official [209a446e3]

### System information

Godot v4.5.dev4

### Issue description (+Steps to reproduce)

I encountered a rather interesting bug. When I was creating new scenes by saving nodes into separate scenes, everything worked fine - until an error appeared in the menuMainSettings scene. That’s the scene I was using to extract nodes into new scenes. Apparently, when I changed the class type, something went wrong - though I didn’t realize what exactly, everything seemed like usual.

Because of this, the project stopped working. Then, through the error message, I managed to open this file:
`res://scenes/mainMenu/menuMainSettings.tscn::GDScript_evgvs`.

It turned out to be a full copy of a .gd file. After cleaning it up, everything started working again. But I became curious: how do you delete this, and what even is it?

I didn’t see this script in the scene tree - as far as I understand, it’s something internal to the scene. In the editor, it's displayed as a gear icon with no fill. Also, after cleaning up the file and trying to restart, I couldn’t save this script anymore - and without saving, it just vanished from the editor.

So yeah, strange bug. 😐
I just wanted to share it in case others run into the same thing. I can’t provide more details. As far as I understand, I wasn’t supposed to even see GDScript_evgvs - if I’m wrong, please correct me.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

summary: Shadow map not updated for billboard sprites when the camera moves

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.4.1 stable and 4.5.dev4 snapshot



### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable (49a5bc7b6) - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3090 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6636) - AMD Ryzen 9 5900X 12-Core Processor (24 threads)

### Issue description

When using OmniLight and Spotlight shadows with billboarded `Sprite3D` (see #72638 and related issues), moving and rotating the camera in the scene does not update the shadows until the sprite is moved.

AFAICT, this is caused by the caching described in https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/105763#issuecomment-2831221427

### Steps to reproduce

The MRP rotates the camera around a `Sprite3D` with `Y-Billboard` enabled (the `Billboard` option also exhibits the same issue). The shadows that it casts on the ground from the `OmniLight3D` are not updated correctly. The sprite also ends up clipping through its own shadow, visible as a dark blob that appears over it at certain angles relative to the light source(s).

There is a checkbox that enables the Sprite3D to move very slightly (0.0001 units) every frame. Doing this "wiggle" is enough to force the shadow map cache to be invalidated, and it works around both issues described above.

summary: OpenXR: Add core support for Khronos loader

description.text: This PR adds support for the Khronos OpenXR loader to the core of Godot. This means that as long as the headset supports it, and you don't use any vendor features or have features locked behind vendor specific manifest entries, Godot can export to a headset without needing the vendor plugin. summary: Window's black border does not scale properly when resizing the window on Compatibility renderer

description.text:
### Tested versions

**Reproducible in:** Godot latest commit [99f5a3d665cfcc517b0b4b55d8668c2116effb69] as of writing, with GLES3 (Compatibility).

**Not reproducible in:**  Godot [f8f350a32a1ebf4114cef6b4b6224089e0abaf74] and older.

### System information

Linux (Arch Linux KDE) - Godot source - GLES3 (Compatibility) - CPU: Ryzen 5 5500U - GPU: Tested individually with both NVIDIA RTX 3050 and Radeon VEGA 6

### Issue description

Resizing the window produces incorrectly sized black borders in the Compatibility renderer when the stretch mode is set to `canvas_items` or `viewport`. For example, a project with a viewport size of 1280 x 720 (16:9) should produce black bars that maintain the 16:9 aspect ratio of the viewport. However, in this demonstration, the black borders cover the viewport and distort the aspect ratio.

1. Godot latest commit `(as of writing `99f5a3d665cfcc517b0b4b55d8668c2116effb69`)` (Unexpected behaviour)

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fe179a85-ab5b-4e11-affc-98a222bcded5

2. Godot [f8f350a32a1ebf4114cef6b4b6224089e0abaf74] (Expected behaviour)

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2556c944-4319-49c7-a02b-ed06ce428ebc

Tested on Linux, Windows, and Web export build; all gave the same result.

This bug is present after #106267 is merged [a0e3ade99f7525bacbc95077cead400676aa2bd4]. I'm unsure of the exact cause, but it's likely related to the `screenrect` size calculation.
```
Vector2 p1 = Vector2(flip_x ? screen_rect_end.x : p_screen_rect.position.x, flip_y ? screen_rect_end.y : p_screen_rect.position.y);
Vector2 p2 = Vector2(flip_x ? p_screen_rect.position.x : screen_rect_end.x, flip_y ? p_screen_rect.position.y : screen_rect_end.y);
Vector2 size = p2 - p1;
Rect2 screenrect = Rect2(p1 / p_screen_rect.size, (size) / p_screen_rect.size);
```

### Steps to reproduce

1. Make a new project with Compatibility Renderer
2. Project Settings -> Window -> Stretch -> Mode -> `canvas_items` or `viewport`
3. Launch the project and resize the window

summary: Make `settings_changed` signal less emitted

description.text:
When testing another PR it kind of annoyed me that changing project's title will update 3D editor's viewports with every letter you type. I looked into it and discovered that:
- ProjectSettings will try to emit the signal at launch for every defined setting (fortunately it's limited to once per frame)
- The signal is emitted every time you change a setting, potentially many times per second
- The signal is emitted after a timeout from changing settings
- The signal is emitted every time you save

The last two actually have the same cause (reminder #100244), i.e. the settings are "changed" when saving, which emits changed signal needlessly.

I added a way to block emitting the signal and applied it in GLOBAL_DEF and in ProjectSettings' inspector. Now the signal is only emitted on the save Timer's timeout and when settings are changed from code. The downside is that when you change a setting in the editor, you may not see the effect immediately (e.g. when changing project title).

EditorSettings have similar problem, but almost nothing uses its equivalent signal. Most of the editor uses the notification, which is emitted by the settings dialog.

summary: Implement materialX shader loading

description.text:
Closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/714
Supersedes and initially based on https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/90918

@fire this is how far I've gotten so far. Not all nodes are implemented but they're enough that I can load materials from https://matlib.gpuopen.com/main/materials/all without missing nodes so it should be enough to make this progress public.

It might be worth checking the `image` node for correctness but other nodes should be fine I think.

The main issue is the conversion between standard_surface and godot's shaders. I tried my best by looking at `thirdparty/materialx/libraries/bxdf/standard_surface_to_gltf_pbr.mtlx` but even that doesn't do it quite right. I don't think I can really get much further on that without actually reading what the standard surface model is and understanding how these materials actually get converted into glsl and into spirv on the gpu.

Beyond that, as the README mentions I'd like for it to be possible to edit materialx graphs from within th editor and be able to actually save to materialx. It would also be useful to be able to bake materialx graphs down into whatever it is we bake into.

Finally there's the more niche features of materialx like color management, unit conversion and custom nodes but that's quite a bit further down the road.

summary: Godot visual shaders compile global expressions in arbitrary order, don't compile; uniform nodes are compiled away if used by expressions but not by other nodes

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in: 4.4.1 stable dotnet

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable.mono - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 6600 (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 32.0.13031.3015) - AMD Ryzen 7 7800X3D 8-Core Processor (16 threads)

### Issue description

Visual shader uniforms are inappropriately compiled away when they aren't connected to an output node BUT are used in a global expression.

Additionally, global expressions have no way to have their order defined by a user, so if global expression 1 uses a function from global expression 2, then the compiler will fail

### Steps to reproduce

Issue 1:
1. Create a visual shader
2. Create a shader uniform
3. Create an expression which uses the shader uniform, then connect this global expression to the output node. DO NOT connect the shader uniform to any other nodes.
4. Compiled shader will not recognize the shader uniform used by the expression, leading to the shader not compiling
Connecting the shader uniform to ANY output node will cause the program to correctly compile, indicating that the shader uniform is being improperly optimized away by the compiler.

Issue 2:
1. Create a visual shader
2. Create a global expression which defines a function f1, which calls another function f2: GLOBAL EXPRESSION 1
3. Create a second global expression which defines the function f2: GLOBAL EXPRESSION 2
4. Global expression 1 appears ABOVE global expression 2 in the compiled shader code. This means that f2 is defined AFTER f1, which means f1 will not compile due to f2 being undefined in the file
Moving f1 and f2 into the same global expression fixes this, but if there was a way to define the order of global expressions this would not be necessary

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

summary: Implement `ModifierBoneTarget3D` which can be target of the other `SkeletonModifier3D`s

description.text:
- Fixes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/106741
- Alternative of https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/106838

Add a ModifierBoneTarget3D that uses a SkeletonModifier cycle that is nearly equivalent to BoneAttachment's functionality but omits the override pose and external skeleton functionality.

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/721d2791-9593-4e8f-a21c-8ba7cd5e3f39)

This approach achieves its purpose by placing it between any SkeletonModifier in the SceneTree, without the use of additional signals or enums.

It differs from #106838 in that it does not track changes in the modifier after the target has been processed, so its transform and skeleton's final result can be not matched, but its usage is clear and should be simpler than https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/106838.

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/759fd1b3-e081-4905-8db7-371e3ae2c752)

As long as the target is placed before the IK, the target position is updated before the IK.

FYI property names are matched with BoneAttachment, so we can migrate them by "Change Type" option on the SceneDock.

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ec1230ee-c9bc-48b3-a30a-ce918488f242)

summary: @GlobalScope method hides local method but editor hints otherwise

description.text:

Godot v4.1.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 Laptop - Intel i7-12700H (20 threads)

### Issue description

If a script uses the same method name as a `@GlobalScope` method, the latter is called. However, the editor hints at the local function instead of the `@GlobalScope` function. 

It is also weird that the local method does not override the `@GlobalScope` method, but it can exist without warnings. 

I think the local method should hide the global method and print a warning, so that any new `@GlobalScope` method would not break compatibility if a name collision occurs, while also not encouraging method hiding.

summary: Doctool --gdscript-docs issues with enums

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in v4.5.dev.custom_build [aef0065e0]

### System information

Godot v4.5.dev (aef0065e0) - Manjaro Linux #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Wed, 14 May 2025 14:56:34 +0000 on Wayland - Wayland display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 7900 XT (RADV NAVI31) - AMD Ryzen 9 7900X 12-Core Processor (24 threads)

### Issue description

When generating XML files with `godot --doctool --gdscript-docs`, enums can display the following problems:
* they do not include any description, even when documented (see #105937);
* variables typed as `Array[MyEnum]` will output `enum="MyEnum[]"` instead of `enum="MyEnum"` (in addition to `type="int[]"`);
* crosslinks to enum values are broken (not directly related to XML files, this is more about in-editor help): `[constant SomeClass.SomeEnum.SOME_VALUE]` is formatted as a link, but does not lead anywhere.


### Steps to reproduce

Create a script containing the following:
```gdscript
class_name DocToolIssue
extends Node

## My enum, not showing in exported XML files.
enum MyEnum {
	FIRST_VALUE,  ## An enum value for [enum MyEnum].
	SECOND_VALUE,  ## An enum value for [enum MyEnum].
}

## An array of [enum MyEnum] values. [constant MyEnum.FIRST_VALUE] means something,
## [constant MyEnum.SECOND_VALUE] means another thing.
var some_var: Array[MyEnum] = []
## This one works properly.
var another_var := MyEnum.FIRST_VALUE
```
Generate the XML file with `godot --doctool --gdscript-docs res://`. The resulting XML file contains the following:
```xml
<class name="DocToolIssue" inherits="Node" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:noNamespaceSchemaLocation="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/godotengine/godot/master/doc/class.xsd">
	<brief_description>
	</brief_description>
	<description>
	</description>
	<tutorials>
	</tutorials>
	<members>
		<member name="some_var" type="int[]" setter="" getter="" enum="DocToolIssue.MyEnum[]" default="[]">
			An array of [enum MyEnum] values. [constant MyEnum.FIRST_VALUE] means something, [constant MyEnum.SECOND_VALUE] means another thing.
		</member>
		<member name="another_var" type="int" setter="" getter="" enum="DocToolIssue.MyEnum" default="0">
			This one works properly.
		</member>
	</members>
	<constants>
		<constant name="FIRST_VALUE" value="0" enum="MyEnum">
			An enum value for [enum MyEnum].
		</constant>
		<constant name="SECOND_VALUE" value="1" enum="MyEnum">
			An enum value for [enum MyEnum].
		</constant>
	</constants>
</class>
```

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[doctool-gdscript.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20442394/doctool-gdscript.zip)

The XML file is included in the docs folder. You can also check the broken link for `[constant MyEnum.FIRST_VALUE]` (you might have to make the window or the help area smaller to force it to scroll, otherwise even working links won't appear to do anything).

summary: Development: EditorContextMenuPlugin CONTEXT_SLOT_FILESYSTEM_CREATE does not receive path information when accessing from empty space click context menu of file list

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible at software built from `master` branch.
Causing code at: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/45fc515ae3574e9c1f9deacaa6960dec68a7d38b/editor/filesystem_dock.cpp#L2608

### System information

MacOS 15.5 (24F74)

### Issue description

This issue was discovered while fixing another bug in [this pull request](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/106811).

When clicking the empty space of the file tree, after click the popup menu generated by EditorContextMenuPlugin, the path of that file tree is not passed to the handle callback.

For `CONTEXT_SLOT_FILESYSTEM_CREATE` context, clicking the option at "Create New" sub-popup menu (in stable version), the folder path is passed as the callback parameter. However, for the file list panel, if it does not pass the folder path, users might not have a quick way to get the folder location, making it hard for creating new files.

Causing code (at master branch):
```c++
if (!EditorContextMenuPluginManager::get_singleton()->activate_custom_option(EditorContextMenuPlugin::CONTEXT_SLOT_FILESYSTEM, p_option, p_selected)) {
	EditorContextMenuPluginManager::get_singleton()->activate_custom_option(EditorContextMenuPlugin::CONTEXT_SLOT_FILESYSTEM_CREATE, p_option, p_selected);
}
```
Instead of `current_path`, `p_selected` is passed to the plugin handle. For empty space click on file tree, since nothing selected, the containing folder location will not be passed.

### Steps to reproduce

* Use the mock code below
* Move mouse pointer to the empty space of file list panel of FileSystem dock.
* Right-click, find the test option in the popup menu.
* The console output should be empty array.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Mock code:

`FileSystemCreateNewHelper.gd`.
```gdscript
class_name  FileSystemCreateNewHelper
extends EditorContextMenuPlugin

func _popup_menu(paths: PackedStringArray) -> void:
    self.add_context_menu_item(
        "TestItem..." if paths.size() > 0 else "New TestItem...",
        self.handleCreateNew,
        godot_util.getEditorIcon("Node2D")
    )

static func handleCreateNew(paths: PackedStringArray):
    print_debug(paths)
    pass
```

Add above class's instance in `plugin.gd`:

```gdscript
func _get_plugin_name() -> String: return "TestPlugin"

func _enter_tree()():
    self.filesystem_popup_helper = FileSystemCreateNewHelper.new()
    self.add_context_menu_plugin(
        EditorContextMenuPlugin.CONTEXT_SLOT_FILESYSTEM_CREATE,
        self.filesystem_popup_helper
    )

func _exit_tree():
    if self.filesystem_popup_helper != null:
        self.remove_context_menu_plugin(self.filesystem_popup_helper)
```

summary: Bitmap font renders with trailing padding

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in 4.4.1 stable

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 22631) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3090 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7270) - AMD Ryzen 9 7950X3D 16-Core Processor (32 threads)

### Issue description

When rendering bitmap fonts (.fnt bmfont) some padding is *always* introduced. 

Fonts with `padding= 0, 1, 0, 1` will have the 1px padding removed from before the first character ("H") correctly, But I wonder if that padding just gets added to behind the last character instead ("o") (observe label bounds):

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7e86eb6f-ab1f-46b1-91b6-792582739e21)

Hence I do believe Godot has some say in how it implements the bmfont padding system, since it's not trivial that padding should be added after each character and not before the first one, OR Godot renders the space before but moves the entire font forwards such that the first character's spacing is moved last instead? What I'm trying to say is that it is probably not just a limitation in the BMfont standard.

Constructing a font with `paddding = 0, 0, 0, 0` fixes this but produces a font without any spacing between characters at all:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f270a3e8-8050-4dd9-adae-0b1ea0ab09e2)

One common "fix" seems to be introducing additional offset in each characters' `xoffset` and `xadvance` params. But to my knowledge this only shifts all characters forward and introduces a space before the first character instead:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5b27c2e1-81ea-4551-b812-b7baa78cff41)

I've attached a project demonstrating this on 3 different bitmap fonts.

### Steps to reproduce

* Make a project with a label
* Add any `.fnt` font
* Drag to minimize the bounds of the label
* Observe spacing on either start or end of label

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[fnt-problem.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20421960/fnt-problem.zip)

summary: Crash when playing game after 4.3 -> 4.4.1 upgrade.

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible after upgrading from 4.3 to 4.4.1

### System information

Windows 11, Geforce 2080 super, Forward+

### Issue description

After upgrading my engine version from 4.3 to 4.4.1, when I try to play the game from the engine, There is a brief resizing, and then the game window stops responding and never returns. It can be closed without the engine crashing. No errors are logged. There is a brief moment when the game is launching that the game window attempts to resize and reposition (I am on a 4k monitor, and launching the game in 1080p windowed, so it first launches in the corner of my screen and then fixes itself to center). I believe that this reposition in combination with the new game player window is likely the source of the crash.

After some research, people seemed to have some crashes related to vulkan rendering, so I swapped the render device as a shot in the dark to fix it. In that session, the game window still crashed, and the log showed errors about missing "tex" files which chatgpt thinks is related to shaders. I check that all the textures for my shaders were still in place (I only have 3 shaders). When relaunching the engine after, the engine itself would no longer launch.

Luckily, I did a backup just before and now have my project re-stabilized on 4.3 with vulkan. I will retry the upgrade on 4.5 when it comes out, but if anyone wants to dig into my files 1 on 1 with me I'd be happy to discord call it, they're too large for upload.

### Steps to reproduce

Relaunch a title (mine specifically I suppose) that was worked on in 4.3, in 4.4.1.

Note that I keep my game very stable and bug free (though the development is quite slow for it D:) This project does not result in any errors or crashes in 4.3.

### Tested versions

Reproducible in: 4.4.1 (from git history should be 3.0-master)

### System information

affects all OS

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4060 Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7270) - 13th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-13900HX (32 threads)


### Issue description

Every physics frame all playing AudioStreamPlayer2D/3D do a physics space_state->intersect_point check on their own own position.  (see [```AudioStreamPlayer2D::_get_actual_bus()```](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/aef0065e090bb4ed0b75c6e2e576e0ef26020a5d/scene/2d/audio_stream_player_2d.cpp#L86-L99))
This is done to support the audio_bus_override feature on Area2D/3D.
All games, including games where audio_bus_override is never used, pay this price.

#### When was this added? 
This exists from the conception of the AudioStreamPlayer2D class itself 
Juan implemented it directly in the first version of the player ( 969fa3cc73e3523fe066357b4f45ca0b52353fea 19/06/2017)

####  How bad is it?

I profiled the exclusive time spent doing these calculations:
In my current project, which has around 200+ Area3Ds and about 40+ AudioStreamPlayer3D playing at any given time, this occupies about 1-2% of the Physics-Process time (0.05ms/frame on high end laptop CPU). Thats 40+ physics checks per frame for no reason.

For small projects and projects with a low count of active AudioStreamPlayer2D/3D, this is completely negligible.
For larger projects, especially ones that plan on targeting low-end specs or consoles, the >0.1 ms/frame lost can be impactful.

#### Is the feature worth the price?
It doesn't appear to be used much. [I found a total of 89 usage of this feature on github.](https://github.com/search?q=%22audio_bus_override+%3D+true%22&type=code)
The functionality of audio_bus_override can be implemented with a simple short gdscript, and it solves a problem that isn't encountered often in the wild. 
(How often do you really want to override the bus of ALL stream players entering a specific area and not just specific ones?)


#### Proposal
##### 1. Workaround
Only do the physics-checks if at least 1 area in the active tree/space has the audio_bus_override flag set.
This would remove the cost for all projects except the few that actively use it.

##### 2. Remove the audio_bus_override feature altogether
Yes, I know even proposing to remove a feature might be controversial, but I think it should be considered.
I think that the `audio_bus_override` isn't up to standards with the modern godot guidelines. 
It's not really a frequently occurring issue and it's not complex to implement with a few lines of gdscript. 

The question is whether we want to keep supporting this old feature correctly or not.


### Steps to reproduce

1. Add a Camera2D/3D
2. Add an AudioListener2D/3D
3. Add Area2D/3Ds with collision shapes
4. Add playing AudioStreamPlayer2D/3D 
(place these inside the aforementioned Area3Ds for worse performance)


### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[MPR#106751.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20503147/MPR.106751.zip)

Needs to be run off  [this custom branch](https://github.com/pirey0/godot/tree/i106751) which adds explicit profiling for the issue.

summary: The game exceeds the limit set with the project's max fps option and the project's vsync_mode option set to disabled

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in: Godot 4.5 dev 4

Not reproducible in: Godot 4.4.1

### System information

Godot v4.5.dev4 - Linux Mint 22.1 (Xia) on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - integrated Intel(R) HD Graphics 4400 (HSW GT2) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i3-4160 CPU @ 3.60GHz (4 threads)

### Issue description

The max fps option should limit the fps to the set fps, however, even if the vsync mode  option is disabled, the fps does not show any limit.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a51db129-0888-46b2-9965-4633bfd8ddc1)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4ac65044-02f3-4604-9f5a-c5c759ae1c22)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4603bb4e-a042-4983-bd3c-3abc03390fc4)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/37c35652-ca71-4980-b98d-f9d9ce3415f2)


Same project in godot 4.4.1

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5a6a2c4c-edff-4fc9-bfc9-744891afee99)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a new project
2. Change the max fps option to 60 and change the vsync mode option to disabled
3. create a 2D node
4. Run the current scene

The monitors tab of the debugger panel will show that the fps goes up to more than 60 or whatever the maximum setting is.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A
